title: Mars — a 60-second explainer
script:
[Mars] This is Mars. The fourth planet from the Sun, and the most explored world beyond our own.
[Half of Earth] Mars is small. About half the width of Earth, closer in size to our Moon than to home.
[A familiar day] But a day on Mars feels oddly familiar. It spins once every twenty-four hours and thirty-seven minutes.
[A thin sky] Its atmosphere is wisp-thin, almost all carbon dioxide, yet thick enough for dust storms that swallow the entire planet.
[Why it's red] And the color? Iron in the soil reacted with oxygen long ago. Mars is, quite literally, rusting.
[We're going] Today, robots roam its surface. Tomorrow, the first human footprints may join them.
